content.text: I'll fetch the review comments on PR #601 to see what needs addressing.

input.command: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/601/comments --jq '.[] | {id, path, line, body, user: .user.login, in_reply_to_id}'

content.content:
{"body":"![critical](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/critical.svg)

If `services.git-hosting.repos` is empty (which is the default value), `lib.escapeShellArgs cfg.repos` evaluates to an empty string. This results in a bash syntax error: `for name in ; do`, causing the `git-hosting-init` service to fail on activation.

Additionally, checking `[ ! -d "$repo" ]` before initializing can be problematic if an empty directory already exists (e.g., created manually or via another process). It is more robust to check for the existence of `"$repo/HEAD"` instead.

Using a bash array `repos=( ... )` and checking `[ ! -e "$repo/HEAD" ]` resolves both issues cleanly.

```
      script = ''
        repos=( ${lib.escapeShellArgs cfg.repos} )
        for name in "''${repos[@]}"; do
          repo="${cfg.dataDir}/$name.git"
          if [ ! -e "$repo/HEAD" ]; then
            git init --bare --initial-branch=main "$repo"
          fi

          # Hook scaffolding: post-receive dispatches to post-receive.d/.
          # Only manage the hook if it is absent or already ours (a store
          # symlink) — a hand-written hook is left alone.
          mkdir -p "$repo/hooks/post-receive.d"
          hook="$repo/hooks/post-receive"
          if [ -L "$hook" ]; then
            case "$(readlink "$hook")" in
              /nix/store/*) ln -sfn ${postReceiveDispatch} "$hook" ;;
            esac
          elif [ ! -e "$hook" ]; then
            ln -s ${postReceiveDispatch} "$hook"
          fi
        done
      '';
```","id":3523804157,"in_reply_to_id":null,"line":152,"path":"modules/git-hosting/default.nix","user":"gemini-code-assist[bot]"}
{"body":"![security-high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/security-high-priority.svg) ![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

In NixOS, `services.openssh.extraConfig` appends text directly to `sshd_config`. If a `Match` block is left open, any configuration appended after it (by other modules or the host configuration) will be incorrectly scoped to that `Match` block. To prevent this leak, always terminate the `Match` block with `Match All`.

```
    services.openssh.extraConfig = ''
      Match User ${cfg.user}
        AllowTcpForwarding no
        AllowAgentForwarding no
        X11Forwarding no
        PermitTunnel no
      Match All
    '';
```","id":3523804158,"in_reply_to_id":null,"line":110,"path":"modules/git-hosting/default.nix","user":"gemini-code-assist[bot]"}
{"body":"![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

If the post-receive hook is triggered with empty input (or run manually), `updates="$(cat)"` will be empty, and `printf '%s\n' "$updates"` will output a single blank line to the sub-hooks. This can cause parsing errors in the sub-hooks. Exiting early when `updates` is empty prevents this.

```
  postReceiveDispatch = pkgs.writeShellScript "git-hosting-post-receive" ''
    # Managed by services.git-hosting — do not edit.
    # Drop executable hooks into post-receive.d/ next to this symlink.
    set -eu
    hook_dir="$(dirname "$0")/post-receive.d"
    [ -d "$hook_dir" ] || exit 0
    updates="$(cat)"
    [ -n "$updates" ] || exit 0
    for hook in "$hook_dir"/*; do
      [ -x "$hook" ] || continue
      printf '%s\n' "$updates" | "$hook" "$@"
    done
  '';
```","id":3523804160,"in_reply_to_id":null,"line":33,"path":"modules/git-hosting/default.nix","user":"gemini-code-assist[bot]"}

If `services.git-hosting.repos` is empty (which is the default value), `lib.escapeShellArgs cfg.repos` evaluates to an empty string. This results in a bash syntax error: `for name in ; do`, causing the `git-hosting-init` service to fail on activation.

Additionally, checking `[ ! -d "$repo" ]` before initializing can be problematic if an empty directory already exists (e.g., created manually or via another process). It is more robust to check for the existence of `"$repo/HEAD"` instead.

Using a bash array `repos=( ... )` and checking `[ ! -e "$repo/HEAD" ]` resolves both issues cleanly.

```
      script = ''

input.command:
cat > /tmp/init-empty.sh <<'EOF'
repos=(  )
for name in "${repos[@]}"; do
  repo="/srv/git/$name.git"
  if [ ! -e "$repo/HEAD" ]; then
    git init --bare --initial-branch=main "$repo"
  fi
  mkdir -p "$repo/hooks/post-receive.d"
  hook="$repo/hooks/post-receive"
  if [ -L "$hook" ]; then
    case "$(readlink "$hook")" in
      /nix/store/*) ln -sfn /nix/store/fake "$hook" ;;
    esac
  elif [ ! -e "$hook" ]; then
    ln -s /nix/store/fake "$hook"
  fi
done
EOF
bash -n /tmp/init-empty.sh && bash /tmp/init-empty.sh && echo "empty-repos case: OK"

content.text: The `Match All` terminator is in place. Now let me verify the dispatcher script behavior directly — especially the new empty-input guard:

input.command:
set -e
d=/tmp/dispatch-test; rm -rf $d; mkdir -p $d/post-receive.d
cat > $d/dispatch <<'EOF'
# Managed by services.git-hosting — do not edit.
# Drop executable hooks into post-receive.d/ next to this symlink.
set -eu
hook_dir="$(dirname "$0")/post-receive.d"
[ -d "$hook_dir" ] || exit 0
updates="$(cat)"
[ -n "$updates" ] || exit 0
for hook in "$hook_dir"/*; do
  [ -x "$hook" ] || continue
  printf '%s\n' "$updates" | "$hook" "$@"
done
EOF
chmod +x $d/dispatch
printf '#!/bin/sh\ncat >> %s/received\n' "$d" > $d/post-receive.d/log-hook
chmod +x $d/post-receive.d/log-hook
bash -n $d/dispatch
# empty stdin: hook must NOT run
: | $d/dispatch
[ ! -e $d/received ] && echo "empty input: hook skipped OK"
# real update lines: hook must receive them
printf 'aaa bbb refs/heads/main\n' | $d/dispatch
grep -q 'refs/heads/main' $d/received && echo "non-empty input: hook ran OK"
input.description: Test post-receive dispatcher with empty and real input

input.command:
git add modules/git-hosting/default.nix && git commit -m "git-hosting: harden init script, sshd Match block, and hook dispatcher

Address review feedback on the git-hosting module:

- Use a bash array and check for \$repo/HEAD instead of the directory
  itself, so a pre-existing empty directory still gets initialized and
  an empty repos list is handled cleanly.
- Terminate the sshd Match block with 'Match All' so directives appended
  to sshd_config after this module aren't scoped to the git user.
- Exit the post-receive dispatcher early on empty stdin so sub-hooks
  never receive a lone blank line." && git push

content.text:
All three review comments on PR #601 are addressed, pushed (commit `be44858`), and replied to.

**Changes made in `modules/git-hosting/default.nix`:**

1. **Init script robustness** — the repo loop now uses a bash array (`repos=( ... )`) and checks `[ ! -e "$repo/HEAD" ]` instead of `[ ! -d "$repo" ]`, so a pre-existing empty directory still gets initialized. In my reply I noted the reviewer's "syntax error on empty list" claim was actually incorrect (`for name in ; do` is valid bash), but the array form is clearer regardless.
2. **sshd Match block leak** — added `Match All` after the git-user hardening block so config appended to `sshd_config` by other modules can't get scoped to the git user, and updated the adjacent comment which previously claimed ordering alone prevented the leak.
3. **Dispatcher empty-input guard** — the post-receive dispatcher now exits early when stdin is empty, so sub-hooks never receive a lone blank line.

**Verification:** I evaluated the real flake output for `classic-laddie` — the generated `git-hosting-init` script and the sshd `extraConfig` both render correctly (Match block properly terminated). I also exercised the dispatcher script directly: empty stdin skips hooks, real update lines reach them. `nix flake check --no-build` passes and `nix fmt` reports no formatting changes; the pre-commit hooks (nixfmt, private-key detection) passed on commit.

result:
All three review comments on PR #601 are addressed, pushed (commit `be44858`), and replied to.

**Changes made in `modules/git-hosting/default.nix`:**

1. **Init script robustness** — the repo loop now uses a bash array (`repos=( ... )`) and checks `[ ! -e "$repo/HEAD" ]` instead of `[ ! -d "$repo" ]`, so a pre-existing empty directory still gets initialized. In my reply I noted the reviewer's "syntax error on empty list" claim was actually incorrect (`for name in ; do` is valid bash), but the array form is clearer regardless.
2. **sshd Match block leak** — added `Match All` after the git-user hardening block so config appended to `sshd_config` by other modules can't get scoped to the git user, and updated the adjacent comment which previously claimed ordering alone prevented the leak.
3. **Dispatcher empty-input guard** — the post-receive dispatcher now exits early when stdin is empty, so sub-hooks never receive a lone blank line.

**Verification:** I evaluated the real flake output for `classic-laddie` — the generated `git-hosting-init` script and the sshd `extraConfig` both render correctly (Match block properly terminated). I also exercised the dispatcher script directly: empty stdin skips hooks, real update lines reach them. `nix flake check --no-build` passes and `nix fmt` reports no formatting changes; the pre-commit hooks (nixfmt, private-key detection) passed on commit.
